pattern: Repeatedly adding or subtracting `value` never changes an element's remainder mod value, so the problem reduces to how many elements land in each remainder class.
hints: Adding or subtracting `value` any number of times only ever reaches numbers with the same remainder mod value as the original -- the remainder is the real resource here.
Count how many elements fall into each remainder class 0..value-1; class r can cover the targets r, r+value, r+2*value, ... one at a time.
The answer is the smallest target t whose remainder class has already run out of copies -- equivalently, the minimum over every remainder r of (count[r] * value + r).

edge_inputs.description: mix of negative and positive values with a small divisor
same array, larger divisor changes the grouping entirely
value of 1 means every element can become any non-negative integer
every element already at remainder 0, so 0 itself is unreachable only if none map there -- here none do
negative numbers whose true remainder is not their raw sign
duplicated remainders stacking up in a single class

stress.description: large random array with mixed sign values and a mid-size divisor
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000000000, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    value = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    return [nums, value]
